betterquesting:10.desc:8:
Now this... this is how you show those §6B**keepers§r what's what. 

The Industrial Farm (IF) is a long term, scalable, solution to Crop production. At its most basic, it will grow, harvest, and hydrate your crops, but with several upgrades and tier based size scaling, it is not a simple machine to make use of. The IF has three modes:

§lInput§r - Pulls seeds and sub-soil blocks into the machine from an Input Bus.
§lFarm§r - Grows the seeds and pushes products into an Output Bus.
§lOutput§r - Exports the seeds and undersoil blocks from the machine into an Output Bus.

You can cycle through them in the machine menu by clicking on the mode button.

To start, you should construct the most basic IF possible. Once you've done that, place your seeds (and any relevant sub-soil blocks) into the Input Bus. 

[warn]Seeds that have sub-soil requirements won't be inserted into the farm unless a block of the sub-soil is also available to the machine. The sub-soil block doesn't need to be in the same Input Bus as the seeds for it to work.[/warn]§r§r

Once you've input your seeds (and sub-soil blocks), you can begin using Farm mode to grow your crops.

When you wish to switch out seeds, enter Output mode before re-inserting the new seeds via the Input mode.

The length of your IF is controlled by the tier of your Seed Bed. An MV farm will require MV Seed Beds and therefore can only have one row of Seed Beds. This also means you can only insert one upgrade block (by replacing the wooden frame on the top layer). Each subsequent tier of Seed Bed increases the length of the machine, the base power consumption, the number of upgrades you can install and the number of seeds that can be inserted into the controller.

[note]The power, water and fertilizer consumption of the industrial farm do not scale with the number of seeds inside the farm, so there is no benefit to running the farm under capacity.[/note]

Crops grown in the Industrial Farm are assumed to grow in the following environment:
- They have sky access (§l+2 Nutrient Points§r)§r
- They have 200 water (§l+10 Nutrient Points§r)§r
- They have 20 fertilizer (§l+2 Nutrient Points§r)§r
  - or 200 when using the fertilizing unit (§l+10 Nutrient Points§r)
- The biome at the location of the controller controls the biome tags and humidity with the biome tags added by the env modules (§lup to +28 Nutrient Points§r)
